pilot.pilot_name: Bangladesh ADM2 waterborne outbreak early warning MVP
pilot.outcome_name: Weekly cholera or acute watery diarrhea escalation risk
pilot.outcome_definition: Binary district-week escalation event indicating that reported cholera or acute watery diarrhea activity warrants operational review within the next epidemiological week.
pilot.decision_statement: Prioritize district-level WASH and surveillance review for the next 1 to 2 weeks when environmental pressure and recent signals indicate elevated outbreak risk.
pilot.label_strategy: Use partner or ministry subnational surveillance when available. The repo keeps a DHS proxy sample label set for local development until a DGHS or partner feed is connected.

data_sources.name: GeoBoundaries Bangladesh ADM2
data_sources.notes: Canonical district geometry source for the pilot geography.

data_sources.name: OCHA COD-PS ADM2 Population
data_sources.notes: Population totals used for exposure and density features.

data_sources.name: DHS Bangladesh WASH Indicators
data_sources.notes: Division-level improved water and sanitation indicators propagated to districts.

data_sources.name: Open-Meteo ERA5 Precipitation Archive
data_sources.notes: Used as the current no-key rainfall feed in the IMERG-equivalent ingest.

data_sources.name: DGHS or partner cholera surveillance feed
data_sources.notes: Locked as the target production label feed; sample_data remains the local dev fallback until this connection exists.

data_sources.name: Bundled Bangladesh sample fixtures
data_sources.notes: Deterministic local development fixtures that mirror the locked pilot schema.

YE-003:2026-W12.narrative: Highest risk district: active outbreak signal, destroyed sanitation, Red Sea flooding history.

YE-001:2026-W12.narrative: Ongoing conflict-driven collapse of water and sanitation systems drives critical risk.

HT-001:2026-W12.narrative: Chronic sanitation deficit and active case transmission drive critical risk in Port-au-Prince.

MZ-002:2026-W12.narrative: Coastal flooding and cyclone history make Beira the highest-risk district in Mozambique.

ET-003:2026-W12.narrative: Persistent case signal from prior weeks and rainfall anomaly drive HIGH risk in Amhara.

HT-003:2026-W12.narrative: Flood-prone Artibonite valley and high sanitation deficit drive HIGH alert in Gonaïves.

KE-003:2026-W12.narrative: High surface water index near Lake Victoria and poor sanitation coverage drive HIGH risk.

PK-003:2026-W12.narrative: Elevated case carry-over and flood proxy signal demand attention in Peshawar.

YE-002:2026-W12.narrative: Port city with damaged infrastructure and persistent case signal — HIGH alert maintained.

SD-003:2026-W12.narrative: Active case transmission and coastal flood exposure drive HIGH risk in Port Sudan.

NG-003:2026-W12.narrative: Flood proxy score and persistent sanitation deficit elevate risk in Rivers State.

IN-002:2026-W12.narrative: Flood-prone delta location and lagged case signal keep Kolkata at medium-high risk.

ET-002:2026-W12.narrative: Seasonal rainfall spike and prior-week case counts elevate risk across Oromia.

HT-002:2026-W12.narrative: Lagged case transmission from the capital and poor water access elevate risk.

KE-002:2026-W12.narrative: Elevated rainfall anomaly and lagged case signal drive moderate risk in Mombasa.

PK-001:2026-W12.narrative: Urban density and sanitation infrastructure gaps drive medium risk in Karachi.

SD-002:2026-W12.narrative: High population density and sanitation infrastructure strain elevate risk in Omdurman.

NG-001:2026-W12.narrative: Dense population and above-average rainfall create medium risk conditions in Lagos.

MZ-001:2026-W12.narrative: Post-cyclone rainfall recovery keeps medium alert status in Maputo.

MZ-003:2026-W12.narrative: Moderate WASH access gap and seasonal rains maintain medium risk in Nampula.

IN-001:2026-W12.narrative: Monsoon tail and dense informal settlements maintain medium risk in Mumbai.

SD-001:2026-W12.narrative: Nile flooding risk and moderate WASH gap maintain medium alert in Khartoum.

NG-002:2026-W12.narrative: Moderate WASH access gap and previous-week case signal maintain medium alert level.

IN-003:2026-W12.narrative: Chennai shows low risk with adequate WASH access and below-seasonal rainfall.

PK-002:2026-W12.narrative: Lahore shows low risk this week with stable case counts and normal rainfall.

KE-001:2026-W12.narrative: Nairobi shows low risk this week. Rainfall within seasonal norms and WASH coverage is high.

ET-001:2026-W12.narrative: Addis Ababa maintains low risk with improving WASH coverage and dry conditions.
